pairs.question: What is the Dutch Reformed Church in Namibia (DRCN) and how does it relate to the Dutch Reformed Church in South Africa (NGK)?
pairs.answer: The Dutch Reformed Church in Namibia (DRCN) is a Christian denomination in Namibia and one of ten synods of the Dutch Reformed Church in South Africa (NGK). It is the only NGK synod located outside South Africa and covers all of Namibia except for the Eastern Caprivi Strip.

pairs.question: When did the Dutch Reformed Church begin its mission work in Namibia and when did it become autonomous?
pairs.answer: The Dutch Reformed Church in South Africa began mission work in Namaqualand (present-day Namibia) in the 1880s, and the first congregation in German South West Africa was established in 1898. The DRCN became an autonomous entity in 1957, when it held its first synod.

pairs.question: What are the key statistics and leadership of the DRCN as of the latest available data?
pairs.answer: The DRCN has 44 congregations, 22,500 members (including children), and 60 pastors. Its moderator is Schalk Pienaar, and the general secretary is Clem Marais.

pairs.question: What are the core beliefs and documents adhered to by the DRCN as a synod of the NGK?
pairs.answer: As a synod of the NGK, the DRCN adheres to the Ecumenical creeds (the Apostles' Creed, the Nicene Creed, and the Athanasian Creed) and the Three Forms of Unity (the Belgic Confession, the Heidelberg Catechism, and the Canons of Dort).

pairs.question: How does the DRCN differ from the NGK regarding same-sex unions and LGBT clergy ordination?
pairs.answer: In 2015, the NGK voted to recognise same-sex unions and resolved to ordain LGBT clergy without requiring celibacy. However, the DRCN did not adopt these changes, as Namibia does not recognise same-sex relationships. The DRCN continues to require celibacy and stated, 'We undertake to admit theological candidates who have a same-gender sexual orientation and who live celibate to the ministry.'

pairs.question: What role does the General Synod of the NGK play in the governance of the DRCN?
pairs.answer: The General Synod of the NGK stipulates matters such as Bible translations, liturgical forms, and hymnals for all its synods, including the DRCN.

pairs.question: Since when has the DRCN been a member of the Council of Churches in Namibia?
pairs.answer: The DRCN has been a full member of the Council of Churches in Namibia since 2000.
